M-24-10.title: Advancing Governance, Innovation, and Risk Management for Agency Use of Artificial Intelligence

components.component: Designate a Chief AI Officer (CAIO) within 60 days.
components.details: CAIOs bear primary responsibility for implementing the memorandum and coordinating with other agencies. The memo describes roles, responsibilities, seniority, position, and reporting structures for CAIOs.

components.component: Convene relevant senior officials to coordinate and govern issues tied to the use of AI within 60 days.
components.details: CFO Act agencies must establish an AI Governance Board chaired by the Deputy Secretary and vice-chaired by the CAIO.

components.component: Submit a plan to achieve consistency with the memorandum within 180 days.
components.details: Agencies must submit a plan to OMB and post it publicly on the agency’s website.

components.component: Inventory each AI use case annually.
components.details: Submit the inventory to OMB and post a public version on the agency’s website. Identify safety-impacting and rights-impacting AI.

components.sub_area: Reporting on AI Use Cases Not Subject to Inventory
components.component: Report aggregate metrics about use cases not subject to inventory.
components.details: Report on the number of such cases that impact rights and safety, and their compliance with Section 5(c) practices.

components.component: Develop and release publicly an AI strategy within 365 days.
components.details: The strategy should identify and remove barriers to the responsible use of AI and achieve enterprise-wide improvements in AI maturity.

components.sub_area: Removing Barriers to the Responsible Use of AI
components.component: Remove unnecessary and unhelpful barriers to the use of AI.
components.details: Focus on IT Infrastructure, Data, Cybersecurity, Workforce, and Generative AI.

components.area: Managing Risks from the Use of AI
components.component: Follow minimum practices when using safety-impacting AI and rights-impacting AI.
components.details: The memorandum enumerates specific categories of AI that are presumed to impact rights and safety.

components.area: Managing Risks from the Use of AI
components.component: Manage AI risks in the context of Federal procurement.
components.details: The memorandum establishes a series of recommendations for managing AI risks in procurement.
